# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains preliminary empirical evidence on adoption, content, structure, writing style, and evolution of AI configuration files in open-source GitHub repositories, with detailed qualitative analysis centered on AGENTS.md files.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The repository filtering pipeline reduced an initial pool of 225,582 repositories to a final sample of 49,311 repositories.

## Source Excerpt

“We then excluded archived, disabled, or locked repositories, resulting in a first sample of 225,582 repositories... This resulted in a final sample of 49,311 repositories.”

## Note

This count reflects the authors’ repository selection pipeline before the 10,000-repository work-in-progress subset.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: method
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
For this work-in-progress paper, the authors analyzed a 10,000-repository subset selected with a custom ranking that balanced project popularity and maturity.

## Source Excerpt

“For this work-in-progress paper, we selected 10,000 repositories based on a custom ranking approach that balances the popularity and maturity of the projects.”

## Note

This is a study design detail for the analyzed subset, not the full long-term target corpus.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: method
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The scan covered four AI configuration file types supported by the GitHub Copilot coding agent: Copilot instructions, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and GEMINI.md.

## Source Excerpt

“We cloned all repositories and scanned their default branch to find all types of configuration files that the GitHub Copilot coding agent supports... Copilot instructions, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and GEMINI.md.”

## Note

The study’s adoption results are bounded to these four file formats.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Only 466 repositories, or 5% of the scanned set, had adopted at least one of the four AI configuration file formats considered.

## Source Excerpt

“Only 466 (5%) of the repositories that we scanned had already adopted at least one of the formats that we considered.”

## Note

The percentage is specific to the scanned repository set and the four formats included in the study.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Among repositories with AI configuration files, the main-language counts were 135 TypeScript, 58 Go, 58 Python, 56 C#, 36 Java, 34 JavaScript, 32 C++, 29 Rust, 19 PHP, and 9 C.

## Source Excerpt

“We found AI configuration files in 135 repositories with TypeScript as main language, 58 Go, 58 Python, 56 C#, 36 Java, 34 JavaScript, 32 C++, 29 Rust, 19 PHP, and 9 C.”

## Note

These are counts by main language among repositories where AI configuration files were found, not language shares across all scanned repositories.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: supports
---
Average file lengths differed by format: Copilot instruction files averaged 310 lines (SD 127), CLAUDE.md 287 lines (SD 112), AGENTS.md 142 lines (SD 231), and GEMINI.md 106 lines (SD 65).

## Source Excerpt

“The Copilot instruction files were on average the longest (M = 310 lines, SD = 127 lines), followed by CLAUDE.md files (M = 287, SD = 112). AGENTS.md files had the highest variation (M = 142, SD = 231). GEMINI.md files were the shortest (M = 106, SD = 65).”

## Note

This evidence reports descriptive length statistics by file type only.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: supports
---
AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md were the most common co-occurring pair of AI configuration files, appearing together in 25 repositories.

## Source Excerpt

“We also looked at which files most commonly co-occured, finding that AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md was the most common pair (in 25 repositories).”

## Note

This is a co-occurrence count for file pairs, not an exclusivity claim.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: method
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The RQ2 heading analysis excluded 15 AGENTS.md files created before January 1, 2025 and excluded 5 headingless AGENTS.md files from the heading-structure analysis.

## Source Excerpt

“We excluded 15 AGENTS.md files that were created before January 1, 2025, i.e., before the AGENTS.md convention was introduced.”
“Five repositories contained AGENTS.md files without any heading structure; these were excluded from the analysis.”

## Note

The structure analysis does not cover all AGENTS.md files in the sample because pre-convention files and headingless files were excluded.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: claim-support
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.2, Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The most frequent level-1 or level-2 AGENTS.md heading category was Conventions/best practices, with 50 headings.

## Source Excerpt

“Table 1. Categories of information provided in AGENTS.md files... Conventions/best practices... 50.”

## Note

This count is for heading-category frequency at heading levels 1 or 2, not for total files.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: claim-support
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.2, Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Other frequent AGENTS.md heading categories included Contribution guidelines (48), Architecture and project structure (47), and Build commands (40).

## Source Excerpt

“Contribution guidelines... 48”
“Architecture and project structure... 47”
“Build commands... 40”

## Note

These counts come from the authors’ grouped heading categories in Table 1.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: result
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The authors identified five stylistic dimensions in AGENTS.md convention sections: descriptive, prescriptive, prohibitive, explanatory, and conditional.

## Source Excerpt

“We found that the writing style can be characterized along five stylistic dimensions: descriptive, prescriptive, prohibitive, explanatory, and conditional.”

## Note

The source presents these as observed stylistic dimensions within analyzed sections, not as a standardized taxonomy validated beyond this dataset.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: result
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The authors found no established structure yet for organizing AGENTS.md content, and they observed wide variation in both content and presentation style.

## Source Excerpt

“Our findings indicate that there is no established structure yet for organizing content in AGENTS.md files, and that there is a lot of variation in terms of how context is provided...”
“In summary, AGENTS.md files vary widely in both the information they contain and how it is presented... without a consistent structure.”

## Note

This evidence is bounded because it identifies a lack of stable structure rather than a settled mechanism for effective context engineering.
```

### file: evidence-13.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-13
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Among 155 AGENTS.md files, 77 files (50%) were unchanged after the initial commit, 36 files (23%) changed once, and 32 files (21%) changed between two and seven times.

## Source Excerpt

“To answer RQ3, we first screened the commit histories of all 155 AGENTS.md files and found that 77 (50%) of them had not been changed after the initial commit, 36 (23%) only once, and 32 (21%) between two and seven times.”

## Note

These counts describe commit-history frequency bands across the 155 AGENTS.md files.
```

### file: evidence-14.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-14
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.3, Table 2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
In the 169 annotated commits from AGENTS.md files with at least 10 commits, the most frequent change categories were Add instruction(s) with 78 instances and Modify instruction(s) with 59 instances.

## Source Excerpt

“For this study, we decided to focus on 10 files (6%) with at least 10 commits, which yielded a sample of 169 commits to annotate...”
“Table 2 shows that the most frequent change categories are ‘Add instruction(s)’ and ‘Modify instruction(s)’.”
“Add instruction(s) ... 78”
“Modify instruction(s) ... 59”

## Note

This frequency result is limited to the high-change subset of files with at least 10 commits.
---
